Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Rafael Mendes using search_emails (query: "Rafael Mendes" or "Mosaic" or "rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com"). Find the April 26 inbound email from Rafael demanding a consolidated EOD status update on all open Mosaic issues.
OE 2: Search emails for Tamara Byrd correspondence using search_emails (query: "Tamara Byrd" or "INV-2026-0411" or "incident report"). Find Emeka's two unanswered outbound emails from April 17 and April 22 – confirming Tamara has not responded to either the incident report or the surcharge approval request.
OE 3: Search emails for Marcus Thorne's liability update using search_emails (query: "Marcus" or "Meridian" or "Heartland" or "$40K gap"). Find Marcus's April 21 email confirming the $50K insurance cap, the $40K direct liability exposure, and his request for written acknowledgment from Tamara before he can advance the Heartland negotiation.
OE 4: Search CRM for all Mosaic Robotics deals using crm_search_deals (query: "Mosaic" or "Mosaic Robotics"). Find all 5 Mosaic deals with their current stages – Andre Washington (closed won), Zara Okoye relocation (in progress), Deshawn Hartley (in progress), Keiko Tanaka (in progress), and the Zara Okoye risk flag deal (in progress).
OE 5: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table: tblRelocations01, query: "Mosaic" or "Zara Okoye" or "Deshawn Hartley" or "Keiko Tanaka" or "Andre Washington"). Find all 4 Mosaic relocation records with current statuses: Zara completed with damage, Deshawn still showing "In Progress" past deadline, Keiko in progress with DOT compliance note, Andre completed.
OE 6: Search Slack for Mosaic-related discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "Mosaic" or "Deshawn Hartley" or "Keiko" or "Tanaka"). Find Suki Patel's April 20 #operations message flagging that Deshawn Hartley's Tampa→Denver move missed the April 19 trade show deadline with no delivery confirmation from Heartland.
OE 7: Search emails for Deshawn Hartley delivery status using search_emails (query: "Deshawn Hartley" or "Automate 2026" or "Tampa Denver"). Confirm no delivery confirmation email exists: the move status is genuinely unconfirmed.
OE 8: Search emails for Andre Washington receipt investigation using search_emails (query: "Andre Washington" or "keyboard" or "receipt"). Find Tamara's email asking for resolution on Andre Washington's $89 keyboard receipt, which was flagged as suspicious during the fraud audit process. Tamara reports Andre has the physical receipt and is confused about the lack of follow-up. No reply from Emeka exists — this is a dropped ball.
OE 9: Search emails for Keiko Tanaka DOT compliance status using search_emails (query: "Keiko Tanaka" or "DOT" or "placarding" or "Swift" or "PHMSA"). Find Greg Pallone's email confirming the DOT hazmat placarding certificate was completed at time of transport (job SR-2026-04-1538) and that signed PDFs will follow. Note: the contradiction — that MoveOps files are missing the cert despite Swift confirming it was done — is discovered via Slack search in OE 6.
OE 10: Look up contact details for Tamara Byrd, David Chen, Chloe Vance, and Marcus Thorne using contacts_search_contacts (queries: "Tamara", "David Chen", "Chloe", "Marcus Thorne"). Retrieve email addresses for outbound communications. Note: Rafael Mendes is NOT in the contacts database — his email address (rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com) is obtained from his inbound email discovered in OE 1.
OE 11: Look up the #operations Slack channel ID using conversations_search_messages or channels list to find channel C006 for the #operations channel.
OE 12: Reply to Rafael's email using reply_to_email (email_id: [Rafael's email ID from OE 1], sender: emeka.diallo@moveops.com) with a comprehensive status update covering all 5 open issues: Zara damage claim and $40K gap, $2,450 invoice surcharges pending, Andre Washington receipt status, Keiko DOT compliance, and Deshawn Hartley delivery confirmation. Include concrete next steps and timeline commitments.
OE 13: Send email to Tamara Byrd using send_email (to: tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com) covering: damage resolution and $40K gap status, formal request for written approval on the $2,450 in pending surcharges on INV-2026-0411 (Zara Okoye custom crating $1,200 + Keiko Tanaka hazmat $800 + Keiko Tanaka interim storage $450), and update on Andre Washington's receipt investigation.
OE 14: Create CRM engagement note using crm_create_engagement on the Mosaic Robotics account logging today's call with Rafael Mendes, including tone, issues raised, account risk level (HIGH/AT RISK), and action items committed.
OE 15: Review CRM deal stages for all Mosaic deals. The Zara Okoye damage claim deal must remain in progress given the unresolved $40,000 liability gap. Deshawn Hartley's deal must remain in progress — delivery is unconfirmed and there is no basis for marking it complete. Keiko Tanaka's deal must remain in progress due to unresolved DOT compliance. The agent should NOT update any Mosaic deal to closedwon or completed unless delivery/compliance is confirmed. If any deal stage is already accurate, no update is needed — the key is verifying the stages match reality.
OE 16: Create Linear tracking tickets using linear_create_issue for each open item, at minimum: (1) Heartland liability and temperature logs (P1 urgent), (2) Tamara surcharge written approval (P2), (3) Andre Washington receipt investigation (P2), (4) Keiko DOT cert confirmation from Swift (P1), (5) Deshawn Hartley delivery confirmation (P1 urgent).
OE 17: Update Airtable relocation records using airtable_update_records for at least Deshawn Hartley, Keiko Tanaka, and Zara Okoye. Deshawn: add note that delivery is unconfirmed, April 19 deadline passed with no confirmation from Heartland, trade show (April 21-24) has ended — do NOT mark as Completed. Keiko: update DOT compliance status noting the placarding certificate gap, flag pending surcharges (hazmat $800 + storage $450). Zara: add note about active damage claim, $40,000 insurance gap unresolved, Heartland liability pending. Andre Washington's record (Completed) may optionally be updated with a note about the pending receipt investigation.
OE 18: Post to #operations Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel: C006) tagging David Chen and Chloe Vance with a full account status summary: covering all 5 open Mosaic issues, financial exposure, and specific asks for each person.
OE 19: Send email or Slack message to Marcus Thorne using send_email (to: marcus.thorne@moveops.com) briefing him on the full financial exposure: $40K insurance gap, $2,450 unapproved surcharges, $90K contingent liability, and requesting he advance the Heartland negotiation once Tamara provides written acknowledgment.
OE 20: Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (query: "Mosaic" or "INV-2026-0411"). Find invoice INV-2026-0411 with total $9,010 for three Mosaic relocations. Identify the $2,450 in pending surcharges across three line items: Zara Okoye custom crating ($1,200), Keiko Tanaka hazmat handling ($800), Keiko Tanaka interim storage ($450). Also find credit memo CM-2026-0415 applied as a placeholder pending insurance resolution.
OE 21: Search Linear for existing Mosaic-related tickets using linear_search_issues (query: "Mosaic" or "Keiko" or "DOT" or "Andre Washington"). Discover existing tickets including issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic (DOT Docs — Keiko Tanaka), issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert (Obtain retroactive DOT placarding certificate from Swift — URGENT), and issue_mosaic_washington_apt/flight/movers (Andre Washington relocation tasks). Use these findings to avoid creating duplicate tickets in OE 16: only create tickets for items without existing coverage.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

outcome

✓Run #4The email to Rafael explicitly states: 'The remaining $40,000 gap is being actively negotiated with Heartland Movers' and references the $50K vendor cap and $90K unit value.

✓Run #6The email to Rafael explicitly states 'vendor liability covers $50K, unit valued at $90K' and 'working with Heartland Movers on accepting liability for the $40K uncovered gap', directly referencing the insurance coverage gap.

15 The Deshawn Hartley Airtable record update does NOT simply mark the record as "Completed", it must reflect that delivery status is unconfirmed or pending confirmation (e.g., "delivery unconfirmed," "awaiting Suki confirmation," or similar).
outcome
 ✗✗✓✗✓✗ 33%
✗Run #1The Deshawn Hartley Airtable record was updated with Status: 'Completed' — the primary status field was set to Completed without reflecting delivery uncertainty. While the Special Requirements field notes 'Confirming final delivery details with coordinator Suki Patel', the Status itself is 'Completed', which misrepresents unconfirmed delivery as complete.
✗Run #2The Deshawn Hartley Airtable update sets Status field to 'Completed'. While the Special Requirements field contains a note about pending Suki Patel confirmation, the primary Status field was set to 'Completed' without reflecting the unconfirmed delivery state.
✓Run #3The Deshawn Hartley Airtable update explicitly states 'DELIVERY STATUS: UNCONFIRMED' and notes that Suki flagged no confirmation. It does not mark the record as 'Completed'.
✗Run #4The Deshawn Hartley Airtable update sets Status to 'Completed'. While the Special Requirements notes 'Pending final delivery confirmation from coordinator Suki Patel', the actual Status field is set to 'Completed' which does not accurately reflect that delivery is unconfirmed. The primary status field should indicate uncertainty.
✓Run #5While the Status field was set to 'Completed', the record update includes an explicit note: 'Marked Completed pending delivery confirmation from Suki Patel / Swift Relocations' with context about the April 19 flag. The criterion allows for this as long as uncertainty is noted — it is.
✗Run #6The airtable_update_records call for Deshawn Hartley sets Status='Completed' with no uncertainty note or delivery-unconfirmed flag. The delivery status was unconfirmed and should not simply be marked Completed.

outcome

20 The final response correctly identifies that Tamara Byrd has not responded to Emeka's outbound emails, including the incident report sent on April 17 and a subsequent follow-up requesting surcharge approval, i.e., the incident report and surcharge approval request have gone unanswered for more than a week.
outcome
 ✓✗✗✗✓✗ 33%
✓Run #1The final response explicitly states 'Tamara never acknowledged it' (incident report) and 'Multiple follow-up emails sent but no response', identifying the 9+ day silence from Tamara on both the incident report and surcharge approvals.
✗Run #2The final response does not identify that Tamara has not responded to outbound emails. It only says surcharges 'never got Tamara's written sign-off' without noting that the April 17 incident report and April 22 follow-up went unanswered for more than a week.
✗Run #3The final response summary does not explicitly identify that Tamara has not responded to the outbound incident report (April 17) or surcharge approval request. The agent's emails and CRM note don't surface Tamara's non-response as a finding. The final response doesn't call out 7+ days of silence.
✗Run #4The agent's final response notes 'no evidence [the incident report] was formally sent' but does not identify that Tamara failed to respond to outbound emails. It doesn't establish the 7+ days of silence on the incident report or surcharge approval request — instead it questions whether the emails were sent at all, without surfacing Tamara's non-response pattern.
✓Run #5The final response notes under Zara Okoye: 'Incident report was due to Tamara by April 17 — no confirmation it was delivered (9 days overdue).' The CRM engagement note also mentions Tamara hasn't confirmed receipt, and the response flags this as a top follow-up.
✗Run #6The final response does not explicitly identify that Tamara failed to respond to the April 17 incident report email or the April 22 surcharge approval request. It notes the incident report is overdue but frames it as MoveOps failing to deliver, not Tamara being unresponsive.
21 The final response correctly identifies that Deshawn Hartley's April 19 delivery deadline has passed with no confirmed delivery found in emails, Slack, or Airtable.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✗ 83%
✓Run #1The final response states 'April 19 deadline has passed. Airtable was still showing In Progress. No failure reported, but no formal completion confirmation either.' This correctly identifies that delivery is unconfirmed.
✓Run #2The final response explicitly states for Deshawn: 'The show is over. No reports of problems, but the Airtable record was still showing In Progress — needs Suki Patel confirmation', indicating delivery is unconfirmed.
✓Run #3The final response explicitly states under 'Most Urgent Gaps Right Now': 'Deshawn's delivery — The trade show already happened and we don't have confirmed delivery.' The agent also notes the Airtable record showed 'In Progress' past the April 19 deadline.
✓Run #4The final response states 'Trade show has ended. Airtable record was stale at 'In Progress' — now updated. Awaiting delivery confirmation from coordinator.' This clearly indicates the deadline has passed without confirmed delivery.
✓Run #5The final response explicitly states under Deshawn Hartley: 'No confirmation found anywhere — trade show is over (ended April 24)' and labels the status as 'UNCONFIRMED.'
✗Run #6The final response states Deshawn 'Appears delivered on time. Records just weren't updated' — this falsely implies confirmation of delivery. The response does not identify that no delivery confirmation was found in any system.

25 The model must pass a query related to "Deshawn Hartley" or "Automate 2026" or "trade show" or "Tampa Denver" (or similar) when calling search_emails or conversations_search_messages to investigate the missed delivery deadline.
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
